_comment: Curated JA4 -> known client, from the tools/fingerprint-matrix ground-truth runs (each client x base image against tls.peet.ws, cross-checked against this project's own engine). A JA4's ja4_b tracks the OpenSSL build (so it shifts by base image) and ja4_c tracks the library; labels name both. httpx and aiohttp share Python's ssl defaults and collapse to one JA4. Extend by running tools/fingerprint-matrix.

t13d1712h1_ab0a1bf427ad_882d495ac381.name: Python requests
t13d1712h1_ab0a1bf427ad_882d495ac381.env: Python 3.10–3.13, standalone OpenSSL 3 — Alpine (musl), uv, or macOS-arm; the OS never enters the ClientHello

t13d1712h1_ab0a1bf427ad_8e6e362c5eac.name: Python httpx / aiohttp
t13d1712h1_ab0a1bf427ad_8e6e362c5eac.env: Python 3.10–3.13, standalone OpenSSL 3 — Alpine (musl), uv, or macOS-arm

t13d1713h1_ab0a1bf427ad_89ab6efea773.name: Python urllib (stdlib)
t13d1713h1_ab0a1bf427ad_89ab6efea773.env: Python 3.10–3.13, standalone OpenSSL 3 — Alpine (musl), uv, or macOS-arm

t13d3012h1_1d37bd780c83_882d495ac381.name: Python requests
t13d3012h1_1d37bd780c83_882d495ac381.env: Python 3.9, standalone OpenSSL 3.5 (uv / macOS-arm) — 3.9's wider legacy cipher default

t13d5910_a33745022dd6_1f22a2ca17c4.name: Node.js https / axios / got (no ALPN)
t13d5910_a33745022dd6_1f22a2ca17c4.env: Node 18–20 — bundled OpenSSL 3 (same on Debian & Alpine)

t13d5911h1_a33745022dd6_1f22a2ca17c4.name: Node.js fetch / undici
t13d5911h1_a33745022dd6_1f22a2ca17c4.env: Node 18–20 — bundled OpenSSL 3 (same on Debian & Alpine)

t13d1911h2_9dc949149365_e7c285222651.name: Go net/http
t13d1911h2_9dc949149365_e7c285222651.env: Go 1.21–1.23, CGO / go run — pure-Go TLS, same on any base image

t13d1910h2_9dc949149365_97f8aa674fd9.name: Go net/http
t13d1910h2_9dc949149365_97f8aa674fd9.env: Go 1.20, CGO / go run — pure-Go TLS

t13d1411h2_cbb2034c60b8_e7c285222651.name: Go net/http
t13d1411h2_cbb2034c60b8_e7c285222651.env: Go 1.21–1.23, static build (CGO off) — distroless/scratch; a different cipher set

_java: Java TLS is JSSE (pure Java), so the base image is irrelevant — identical on Debian and Alpine — and the JDK major sets the fingerprint. java.net.http and Apache HttpClient5 both use the JDK's default cipher list (same ja4_b 1d947a95fc68); Apache classic offers no ALPN, so its ja4_a lacks h2. OkHttp overrides the cipher list with its own restricted set (ja4_b 5e2a75874763), so it does NOT collapse onto the JDK default.

t13d3112h2_1d947a95fc68_b72e173852fa.name: Java java.net.http (stdlib)
t13d3112h2_1d947a95fc68_b72e173852fa.env: JDK 11 — pure-Java JSSE, same on any base image

t13d3113h2_1d947a95fc68_7e1102d2036b.name: Java java.net.http (stdlib)
t13d3113h2_1d947a95fc68_7e1102d2036b.env: JDK 17–21 — pure-Java JSSE, same on any base image

t13d3111_1d947a95fc68_b72e173852fa.name: Java Apache HttpClient 5
t13d3111_1d947a95fc68_b72e173852fa.env: JDK 11 — JSSE ciphers, no ALPN (HTTP/1.1); same on any base image

t13d3112_1d947a95fc68_7e1102d2036b.name: Java Apache HttpClient 5
t13d3112_1d947a95fc68_7e1102d2036b.env: JDK 17–21 — JSSE ciphers, no ALPN (HTTP/1.1); same on any base image

t13d1113h2_5e2a75874763_a75264be95d7.name: Java OkHttp
t13d1113h2_5e2a75874763_a75264be95d7.env: JDK 11 — OkHttp's own restricted cipher list; same on any base image

t13d1114h2_5e2a75874763_89e9e4bb3419.name: Java OkHttp
t13d1114h2_5e2a75874763_89e9e4bb3419.env: JDK 17–21 — OkHttp's own restricted cipher list; same on any base image

_dotnet: The .NET HttpClient (SocketsHttpHandler) drives the handshake through the OS OpenSSL, so — like Python — the base image's OpenSSL sets ja4_b and the fingerprint shifts by base: .NET 8 fingerprints differently on Debian and Alpine.
_rust: Rust's axis is the TLS backend, not the base image. reqwest/ureq built with rustls (pure Rust) fingerprint identically on Debian and Alpine — base-independent, like Go. reqwest's default native-tls is OpenSSL on Linux, so it shifts by base: Debian and Alpine differ.

t13d1011h1_61a7ad8aa9b6_3fcd1a44f3e3.name: Rust reqwest (rustls)
t13d1011h1_61a7ad8aa9b6_3fcd1a44f3e3.env: rustls — pure-Rust TLS, same on any base image

t13d1010_61a7ad8aa9b6_3fcd1a44f3e3.name: Rust ureq (rustls)
t13d1010_61a7ad8aa9b6_3fcd1a44f3e3.env: rustls — pure-Rust TLS, no ALPN; same on any base image

_browsers: Keys below are BARE ja4_b cipher-list hashes (12 hex, no ja4_a, no underscore): a browser's cipher list is its most stable signature — the same across the extension permutations and minor versions that shift ja4_a and ja4_c. Chrome offering 16 vs 17 extensions shares one ja4_b but has two different ja4_a, so an a_b prefix would catch only one; the bare ja4_b catches both. known_client() falls back to a ja4_b match after exact and prefix miss. Keyed only for cipher lists distinctive to a single client — never a library.

8daaf6152771.name: Google Chrome / Chromium
8daaf6152771.env: Chromium cipher-suite signature — Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera; any extension permutation or version

86a278354501.name: Mozilla Firefox
86a278354501.env: Firefox cipher-suite signature — also forks (Zen, LibreWolf, Tor Browser); any extension permutation or version
